mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding household income and lower recession risk, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above its recent low signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession probability, causing lower expected earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp from its trailing 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is accelerating while the federal funds rate is also rising, tighter real financial conditions compress equity valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS increases over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS tightening signal does not underperform unconditional 20-day returns by at least 25bp on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening credit-sensitive demand and future construction-linked employment losses, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST year-over-year contractions greater than 15% are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production over the prior three months signals improving goods-sector output and operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations are revised up.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month momentum has a negative average 5-day forward S&P 500 return in walk-forward out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales signals resilient consumer spending and near-term revenue strength for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth fails to deliver a positive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts contract while payrolls continue to expand, the economy is in a late-cycle rollover where equity earnings expectations lag the housing shock, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% year over year while PAYEMS is positive over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal does not produce lower 60-day returns than either HOUST-only or PAYEMS-only signals in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate cuts combined with rising unemployment identify a growth scare rather than benign easing, so risk premia rise and the S&P 500 delivers negative returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5pp over 3 months and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or outperform unconditional 5-day returns by more than 10bp on average.
